Key verification points:
- **Coverage**: exactly 150 RP18 units (RP18.P00.M00.S01–RP18.P01.M02.S08); deliverable and micro-title distributions both reconcile to 150; section row titles match the registry's `required_section_rows`.
- **Descriptor-only boundaries hold**: every row is `descriptor_only=true` / `runtime_execution=false`; the no-write attestation zeroes all runtime/write/storage/Hermes-receipt flags; `synthetic_only`/`no_real_data` true; no I/O in any source file.
- **Authority boundary intact**: `claude_is_final_approval=false`, `read_only=true`, tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob; no enterprise-trust claim from local validation.
- **Conservative production posture**: manifest reports `production_ready=false`, `runtime_ready=false`, `implementation_layer="descriptor"` — no over-claim.

- **Wiring + scope hygiene**: `rp18:ai-legal-workflows-core:validate` registered and tests included; unrelated dirty files correctly excluded.

VERDICT: PASS, no findings, does not block pack or goal closeout.

Coverage verified against docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json and the review baseline: exactly 150 RP18 units spanning RP18.P00.M00.S01–RP18.P01.M02.S08, all program_id=RP18, no duplicate IDs. Deliverable distribution reconciles to 150 (implementation 108, contract 8, security_audit 16, hermes_evidence 7, claude_review 3, ui 8) and micro-title counts reconcile to 150 across the 14 descriptor sections (P00 M00–M10 scope-foundation cycle + P01 M00–M02 model-foundation head). Section row titles match required_section_rows for every micro-phase in packages/ai-legal-workflows/src/registry.js.

Descriptor-only / no-write / no-real-data boundaries hold. Every generated row carries descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false (service.js). The NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION and the result freeze in registry.js/service.js set all runtime, permission-evaluation, audit-write, DB, object-storage, command-runtime, Hermes-runtime-receipt, and real-fixture flags to false, with synthetic_only/no_real_data true and the seven no-leak guards present. The src modules contain no filesystem writes, network, or DB I/O; the test and validator only Read files and assert. contracts/ai-legal-workflows-core-contract.json embeds program, current_pack, both attestation copies, the full scope_contract_foundation_descriptor (row-extras match service ROW_EXTRAS), cp551_requirements, and historical_pack_bindings ending at CP00-551 — consistent with the validator's deepEqual drift checks.

Authority boundary respected: descriptor.authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, claude_packet.read_only=true with allowed_tools=[Read,Grep,Glob]. Production posture is correctly conservative — manifest.json reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer="descriptor", status="implemented_pending_review"; the production_ready_flag is descriptor-verification only, not a runtime/enterprise-trust claim. Hermes is used solely for a deterministic descriptor evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime.

Review-hardening (CP178+) checks pass: readiness-report.json auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1432-byte well-formed JSON stdout, 0 stderr); no forbidden evidence patterns (no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, or missing fields); review_hardening.invalid_attempts_preserved=[] for this pack. Wiring confirmed: package.json registers rp18:ai-legal-workflows-core:validate and npm test includes packages/ai-legal-workflows/test/*.test.js. Scope hygiene confirmed: unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook docs, .docx) are correctly excluded per changed-file-scope.json and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted=[]. Note: npm run validate (validate-product-contract.mjs) validates at the module level (legal-knowledge-ai) and does not enumerate individual RP programs, so RP18's absence there is by-design, not a gap.
